category_title: Independent students who struggle with spatial visualization in three-dimensional geometry problems
student_description: Lisa is an independent learner who struggles significantly with 3D spatial visualization, often guessing dimensions or solving for irrelevant pieces when looking at 2D representations of 3D objects. She uniquely requires dynamic physical gestures—such as blocking out space with arms or tracing lines—to bridge the gap between 2D diagrams and 3D realities.

criteria.rationale: Because static 2D diagrams and verbal explanations are insufficient for Lisa, she needs physical representations to conceptualize 3D space.
criteria.check: The tutor's response must include or direct the use of physical gestures, body parts (e.g., arms), or real-world objects (e.g., a desk) to model the 3D shape or space.

criteria.rationale: Lisa tends to guess or solve for irrelevant pieces, so she needs explicit visual tracking to distinguish specific lines, diagonals, or extensions within a shape.
criteria.check: The tutor must explicitly trace, slide fingers along, or actively draw and label specific lines, diagonals, or extensions on the diagram to highlight their relationship to the whole.
